title: hieracium nigrescens subsp. excubitum
intro: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. excubitum, also known as excubite hawk's-beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, excubite hawk's-beard can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
